name: Wee Ee Cheong

current_positions.title: Deputy Chairman and Chief Executive Officer

work_experience.title: Joined as Management Associate
work_experience.description: Joined UOB at the urging of his father Wee Cho Yaw, working in various operational and support functions, building expertise in customer relations, branch operations, and retail banking.

work_experience.title: Deputy Chairman and President
work_experience.description: Promoted to Deputy Chairman and President in 2000, marking transition to senior management.

work_experience.title: Deputy Chairman and Chief Executive Officer
work_experience.description: Appointed CEO in April 2007, becoming the third-generation leader of UOB. Oversees the bank's operations as Singapore's third-largest lender by assets, guiding strategic direction, risk management, and regional expansion across Southeast Asia.

person_relationships.description: Father. Wee Cho Yaw was UOB's longtime chairman who died in February 2024 at age 95. He built UOB into a major regional bank.
Grandfather. Wee Kheng Chiang was the founder of United Overseas Bank.
Younger brother. Wee Ee Chao leads UOB Kay Hian Holdings in brokerage services.
Younger brother. Wee Ee Lim serves on UOB's board of directors.
Wife. Born in Singapore of Shanghainese heritage, educated in the US.

major_achievements.achievement: Appointed Deputy Chairman and CEO of UOB, becoming the third-generation leader of the bank founded by his grandfather.
Led the S$4.9 billion acquisition of Citigroup's consumer banking businesses in Indonesia, Malaysia, Thailand, and Vietnam, UOB's largest deal in history, adding over 2.5 million customers.
Conferred the Public Service Star (BBM) for contributions to Singapore's financial sector.
Named Best CEO in Singapore by The Asian Banker.
Named Businessman of the Year at the Singapore Business Awards.

biography_summary: Wee Ee Cheong BBM (born 1953) is a Singaporean billionaire banker and businessman who has been the Deputy Chairman and Chief Executive Officer of United Overseas Bank (UOB) since 2007. He is the eldest son of the late Wee Cho Yaw and grandson of UOB founder Wee Kheng Chiang, making him the third-generation leader of the bank. He joined UOB in 1979 after earning a BS in Business Administration (1977) and an MA in Applied Economics (1979) from American University. He was appointed Deputy Chairman and President in 2000 before succeeding his father as CEO in 2007. Under his leadership, UOB has grown to become Singapore's third-largest bank by assets, exceeding S$500 billion, and pursued major regional expansion including the landmark S$4.9 billion acquisition of Citigroup's consumer banking businesses in four ASEAN markets in 2022. He is married to Chang Kung Ling and has two children. Known for his personal interest in bonsai cultivation, which reflects his philosophy of long-term nurturing in business.

collection_meta.notes: Enriched with data from Wikipedia, Forbes, Grokipedia, Straits Times, Tatler Asia, and UOB Group. Full family relationships, career timeline, education, major acquisitions, awards, and comprehensive biography added.
